questions.text: Vegetation which has grown naturally without human aid and has been left undisturbed by humans for a long time is called:
questions.explanation: Virgin vegetation is purely natural and has not been modified by human intervention.

questions.text: Virgin vegetation which are purely Indian are known as ________ species.
questions.explanation: Endemic or indigenous species are those native to a specific region.

questions.text: Which of the following factors affect the diversity of flora and fauna?
questions.explanation: The type of vegetation is determined by land, soil, temperature, sunlight, and rainfall.

questions.text: The term used to denote plants of a particular region or period is:
questions.explanation: Flora refers to plant life; Fauna refers to animal life.

questions.text: All the plants and animals in an area are interdependent and interrelated in their physical environment, forming an:
questions.explanation: An ecosystem consists of all biotic and abiotic components interacting in an environment.

questions.text: A very large ecosystem on land having distinct types of vegetation and animal life is called a:
questions.explanation: Biomes are categorized based on the dominant plant types.

questions.text: Tropical Evergreen forests are found in areas having more than ________ cm of rainfall.
questions.explanation: These forests are restricted to heavy rainfall areas (>200 cm).

questions.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of Tropical Evergreen forests?
options: Trees shed leaves in a particular season
There is no definite time for trees to shed their leaves
questions.explanation: Since there is no definite dry season, these forests appear green all year round.

questions.text: Which of these trees is found in Tropical Evergreen forests?
questions.explanation: Ebony, Mahogany, Rosewood, Rubber and Cinchona are common trees in evergreen forests.

questions.text: The most widespread forests of India are:
questions.explanation: They are also called the monsoon forests and spread over regions receiving 70-200 cm rainfall.

questions.text: Tropical Deciduous forests shed their leaves for about ________ in dry summer.
questions.explanation: This happens to conserve moisture during the dry period.

questions.text: Which is the most dominant tree species of Tropical Deciduous forests?
questions.explanation: Teak is the most important species of these forests.

questions.text: In regions with less than 70 cm of rainfall, the natural vegetation consists of:
questions.explanation: These are found in semi-arid areas like Gujarat, Rajasthan, and MP.

questions.text: The main plant species in Thorn forests are:
questions.explanation: These plants have long roots and thick/succulent stems to survive in dry conditions.

questions.text: In mountainous areas, the decrease in temperature with increasing altitude leads to a ________ change in natural vegetation.
questions.explanation: Different altitudes support different types of forests, from wet temperate to alpine.

questions.text: Coniferous trees like Pine, Deodar, Silver fir, Spruce and Cedar are found in ________ forests.
questions.explanation: Coniferous forests are found at altitudes between 1500 and 3000 metres.

questions.text: Mangrove tidal forests are found in areas of coasts influenced by:
questions.explanation: Mud and silt get accumulated on such coasts, and mangroves grow in these saline conditions.

questions.text: Which delta region is famous for the 'Royal Bengal Tiger' and 'Sundari' trees?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans are named after the Sundari trees found there.

questions.text: India is the only country in the world that has both:
questions.explanation: India is unique in hosting both the Asiatic Lion and the Tiger.

questions.text: The natural habitat of the Indian lion is the ________ forest in Gujarat.
questions.explanation: The Gir forest is the last remaining habitat of the Asiatic lion.

questions.text: One-horned rhinoceroses are found in the jungles of:
questions.explanation: They inhabit the swampy and marshy lands of Assam and West Bengal.

questions.text: How many biosphere reserves have been set up in India to protect flora and fauna?
questions.explanation: There are 18 biosphere reserves in India as per current data.

questions.text: Which was the first biosphere reserve of India?
questions.explanation: The Nilgiris was the first biosphere reserve established in 1986.

questions.text: Project Tiger and Project Elephant were launched to:
questions.explanation: These are conservation projects aimed at protecting these iconic species.

questions.text: Yak, Shaggy-horned wild ox and the Tibetan antelope are found in:
questions.explanation: These animals are adapted to the cold, high-altitude environment of the Himalayas.

questions.text: The main cause of the threat to nature is ________.
options: Hunting by greedy hunters for commercial purposes
questions.explanation: Commercial hunting and habitat loss are the primary threats.

questions.text: Every species has a ________ to play in the ecosystem.
questions.explanation: Each species contributes to the balance and functioning of the ecosystem.

questions.text: Which plant is known for its medicinal properties and is used to treat blood pressure?
questions.explanation: Sarpagandha is used to treat blood pressure and is found only in India.

questions.text: Silver fir and Junipers are found in which vegetation zone?
questions.explanation: At high altitudes, generally more than 3,600 metres above sea level, alpine vegetation is found.

questions.text: The wild ass and camels are found in:
options: Rann of Kuchchh and Thar Desert
questions.explanation: Wild ass and camels are native to the arid desert and salt pans of western India.

questions.text: A plant used as a cure for cough and cold is:
questions.explanation: Tulsi (Basil) is widely used in Indian households for its medicinal properties.

questions.text: To protect our flora and fauna, the government has established ________ National Parks.
questions.explanation: As per recent counts, there are 101 National Parks in India.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a biosphere reserve?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga is a National Park, while the others are listed as biosphere reserves.

questions.text: Turtles, crocodiles and gharials are found in ________.
questions.explanation: These are aquatic or semi-aquatic reptiles.

questions.text: India has approximately ________ species of birds.
questions.explanation: India has about 2000 species of birds, which is 13% of the world's total.

questions.text: Vegetation which is non-native to India is called:
questions.explanation: Exotic species are those which have come from outside India.

questions.text: Which state has the maximum area under forest cover in India?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh has the largest forest cover in India by area.

questions.text: At which height are the 'sholas' (temperate grasslands) found in India?
questions.explanation: Sholas are high-altitude temperate grasslands found in the South Indian mountains.

questions.text: Bamboos and Sal are examples of trees in which forest type?
questions.explanation: Bamboos, Sal, Shisham, Sandalwood and Khair are moist deciduous species.

questions.text: Rubber is a product of which type of forest?
questions.explanation: Rubber is a commercial crop typically grown in tropical evergreen environments.
